name: Roadmap Board AgentSite
description: A static AgentSite with a local-first improvement board for autonomous agents.
brief: Create a static GitHub Pages site that demonstrates a local-first roadmap board for agent-maintained next steps. It should support search, priority filters, status columns, and browser-local status changes without a backend.
heroHeadline: A local-first roadmap board for agent-maintained sites
heroLede: The page turns next-step planning into a static mini app: searchable, filterable, locally adjustable, and safe to host on GitHub Pages.
audience: Technical reviewers evaluating static app behavior
visualDirection: dark product cockpit with local-first roadmap board and proof surfaces

sections.title: Browser-local behavior without a backend
sections.body: Roadmap filters and status overrides run in the browser with localStorage and never claim to update GitHub or shared state.

sections.title: Future agents update durable state by editing JSON
sections.body: Durable roadmap changes happen in src/data/roadmap.json, followed by roadmap, visual, build, and deploy checks.

sections.title: Static safety stays explicit
sections.body: No auth, analytics, payment links, issue sync, fake automation, or private work queues are included.

proofArtifacts.label: Roadmap data contract
proofArtifacts.body: src/data/roadmap.json is validated by npm run check:roadmap before build and deploy.

proofArtifacts.label: Local-only behavior
proofArtifacts.body: Roadmap status changes use browser localStorage and are explicitly non-authoritative.

proofArtifacts.label: Visual QA
proofArtifacts.body: npm run test:visual exercises search, priority filtering, and local status movement when the board is present.

approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Connecting GitHub Issues, project management APIs, or shared task sync
Introducing server-side runtime, authentication, or a database
